target.rawSql: SELECT time, timeend, device AS text FROM (SELECT device, min(hour) AS time, max(hour) AS timeend, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY min(hour)) AS rn FROM health.heart_rate_hourly WHERE device IS NOT NULL GROUP BY device) d WHERE rn = 1

SELECT time, timeend, device AS text FROM (SELECT device, min(hour) AS time, max(hour) AS timeend, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY min(hour)) AS rn FROM health.heart_rate_hourly WHERE device IS NOT NULL GROUP BY device) d WHERE rn = 2

SELECT time, timeend, device AS text FROM (SELECT device, min(hour) AS time, max(hour) AS timeend, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY min(hour)) AS rn FROM health.heart_rate_hourly WHERE device IS NOT NULL GROUP BY device) d WHERE rn = 3

SELECT time, timeend, device AS text FROM (SELECT device, min(hour) AS time, max(hour) AS timeend, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY min(hour)) AS rn FROM health.heart_rate_hourly WHERE device IS NOT NULL GROUP BY device) d WHERE rn = 4

panels.title: Heart rate — daily percentile band (p25–p75, 28d smoothed)
panels.description: Median line with p25–p75 band, computed from hourly percentile_agg() sketches rolled up to civil days in $timezone, then smoothed with a 28-day rolling average. Robust to sensor glitches: never raw min/max.

targets.rawSql:
WITH daily AS (
  SELECT
    (hour AT TIME ZONE '$timezone')::date AS day,
    approx_percentile(0.25, rollup(bpm_agg)) AS p25,
    approx_percentile(0.5,  rollup(bpm_agg)) AS median,
    approx_percentile(0.75, rollup(bpm_agg)) AS p75,
    sum(samples) AS samples
  FROM health.heart_rate_hourly
  WHERE $__timeFilter(hour)
  GROUP BY 1
  HAVING sum(samples) >= 30
)
SELECT
  (day::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone') AS "time",
  avg(p25)    OVER w AS p25_28d,
  avg(median) OVER w AS median_28d,
  avg(p75)    OVER w AS p75_28d
FROM daily
WINDOW w AS (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 27 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
ORDER BY day

WITH daily AS (
  SELECT day, bpm FROM health.resting_heart_rate WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
)
SELECT (day::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone') AS "time",
       avg(bpm) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 27 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS "resting bpm (28d avg)"
FROM daily ORDER BY day
SELECT time, weight_kg AS "weight (kg)" FROM health.weight WHERE $__timeFilter(time) ORDER BY time

WITH per_source AS (
  SELECT day, source, sum(minutes_asleep) / 60.0 AS asleep
  FROM health.sleep_session WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
  GROUP BY 1, 2
), daily AS (
  SELECT day, max(asleep) AS asleep FROM per_source GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT (day::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone') AS "time",
       avg(asleep) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 27 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS "hours asleep (28d avg)"
FROM daily ORDER BY day

panels.title: Sleep composition (28d avg minutes per stage)
panels.description: Stages-era nights only (levels_summary present with the stages vocabulary). Classic-era nights don't distinguish deep/light/REM.

targets.rawSql:
WITH per_night AS (
  SELECT day, level, sum(minutes) AS minutes
  FROM health.sleep_composition
  WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp) AND level IN ('deep', 'light', 'rem')
  GROUP BY 1, 2
)
SELECT (day::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone') AS "time", level,
       avg(minutes) OVER (PARTITION BY level ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 27 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS minutes
FROM per_night ORDER BY day

WITH daily AS (
  SELECT day, rmssd_ms FROM health.hrv_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp) AND rmssd_ms IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT (day::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone') AS "time",
       avg(rmssd_ms) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 27 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS "rmssd (28d avg)"
FROM daily ORDER BY day

panels.title: Activity volume (28d avg minutes by intensity)

targets.rawSql:
WITH daily AS (
  SELECT day, 'lightly' AS category, lightly_min AS minutes FROM health.active_minutes_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT day, 'moderately', moderately_min FROM health.active_minutes_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT day, 'very', very_min FROM health.active_minutes_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
)
SELECT (day::timestamp AT TIME ZONE '$timezone') AS "time", category,
       avg(minutes) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 27 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS minutes
FROM daily ORDER BY day

panels.title: Year-over-year — daily steps by day-of-year
panels.description: Each point is one calendar day; x is day-of-year so every year overlays on a common axis, colored by year. A scatter (not connected lines) to keep a decade of overlapping series legible.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  extract(doy from day)::int AS day_of_year,
  extract(year from day)::text AS year,
  steps
FROM health.steps_daily
WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Seasonality — average daily steps by month

targets.rawSql:
SELECT to_char(day, 'Mon') AS month, avg(steps) AS "avg steps"
FROM health.steps_daily
WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamp)
GROUP BY to_char(day, 'Mon'), extract(month from day)
ORDER BY extract(month from day)

panels.title: Correlation: 28d avg steps → weigh-in weight
panels.description: X = trailing 28-day average daily steps ending the day before each weigh-in. Y = weight. Correlation, not causation; color = year.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  w.time AS weigh_in,
  extract(year from w.time)::text AS year,
  (SELECT avg(sd.steps) FROM health.steps_daily sd WHERE sd.day >= (w.time::date - 28) AND sd.day < w.time::date) AS avg_steps_28d,
  w.weight_kg
FROM health.weight w
WHERE $__timeFilter(w.time)
ORDER BY w.time

panels.title: Correlation: previous-day activity → sleep score
panels.description: X = previous civil day's step total. Y = that night's Fitbit sleep score. Correlation, not causation; color = year.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  ss.day AS night,
  extract(year from ss.day)::text AS year,
  (SELECT sd.steps FROM health.steps_daily sd WHERE sd.day = ss.day - 1) AS prev_day_steps,
  sc.overall AS sleep_score
FROM health.sleep_session ss
JOIN health.sleep_score sc ON sc.sleep_log_id = ss.log_id
WHERE ss.main_sleep AND $__timeFilter(ss.day::timestamp)
ORDER BY ss.day

panels.title: Correlation: 28d avg activity → resting heart rate
panels.description: X = trailing 28-day average daily steps ending the day before. Y = that day's resting heart rate. Correlation, not causation; color = year.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  r.day AS day,
  extract(year from r.day)::text AS year,
  (SELECT avg(sd.steps) FROM health.steps_daily sd WHERE sd.day >= (r.day - 28) AND sd.day < r.day) AS avg_steps_28d,
  r.bpm AS resting_hr
FROM health.resting_heart_rate r
WHERE $__timeFilter(r.day::timestamp)
ORDER BY r.day
